notes: Subject of study; enterprise perception: toy computer, lacks applications, connectivity myths.

tech_name: IBM PS/2 and IBM PC Architecture
notes: Over 80% of corporate MIS committed to IBM PC; PS/2 Model 55SX and up cited as client platform.
Most frequently mentioned target for client applications; has established itself as a standard.
Used for multithreading or IBM OfficeVision/2 commitments; described as 'late and slow' by MIS.
Dominant LAN OS; Macintosh support for Netware 286 exists but unknown to MIS; Netware 386 Mac support delayed.
Installed base ~5 million; declining at 15-20% per year through 1993; PC replacement underway.

lifecycle_current: market niche; subsumed by client-server and web
notes: Aberdeen estimates frontware market at under $50M in 1990; IBM blessed Easel Corp as frontware-of-choice.
95%+ of mainframe-size applications still using dumb 3270 terminals; maturity projected for mid-1994.
Departmental communications gateway topology emerging; Aberdeen forecasts rapid growth.
IBM SAA for cooperative processing from PS/2s to CICS/DB2 still 2 years away in 1991; weak customer commitment seen.
IBM-blessed frontware and client development toolset; most frequently mentioned by MIS sites.
Apple-promoted frontware product; only UT Austin using in production; criticized as too complex.

lifecycle_current: evolved; IBM acquired Software AG integration portfolio 2023
notes: Macintosh-supported client CASE tool and 4GL for mainframe host; used by UT Austin.
Native APPC applications planned at Merrill Lynch for 1991; Merrill Lynch expects common use by 1994.
Non-starters for corporate MIS desktops but Unix servers attracting interest for RDBMS decision support.
